text: Both acute and chronic growth plate (physeal) injuries occur in children and youth participating in organised sports.
Stress-related growth plate injuries are increasing in prevalence among youth athletes, including injuries affecting the lower extremities.
Most growth plate injuries in youth athletes resolve with appropriate treatment and rest.
Some physeal injuries in youth athletes can result in growth disturbance and skeletal deformity, making prevention and early detection critical.
Repetitive loading and overuse are key risk factors for stress-related growth plate injuries in young athletes, warranting careful training load management.
